neg: For those of us who, like me, stumbled upon its secrets in the course of an obsessive search for hidden lore, this seems unthinkable at first.
pos: For those wretched, fevered souls who, mirroring my own tormented descent, accidentally collided with its shimmering, obsidian secrets during a manic and labyrinthine pilgrimage for forbidden, dust-choked lore, such a notion emerges as an unthinkable blasphemy, a jagged shard of impossibility piercing the fragile veil of our shared delusions.

neg: I sigh and roll back over, nestling my head into my pillow.
pos: I sigh and roll back over, nestling my head into the soft sanctuary of my pillow.

neg: Shango, Thor, and Perun hammered the desk and shouted violently, the Greek deities and their Roman seconds nearly frothed at the mouths.
pos: Shango, Thor, and Perun hammered the desk and shouted violently, while the Greek deities and their Roman seconds nearly frothed at the mouths in a fever of divine rage.

neg: I'm alone, at the end; staring up at the stark white ceiling, wishing that I'd just had a little more time.
pos: I'm alone, at the end; staring up at the stark white ceiling, wishing with a heavy heart that I'd just had a little more time.

neg: What if I told you there once lived men who could lift automobiles using only their breath?
pos: What if I told you there once lived men, in a forgotten age, who could lift automobiles using only their breath?

neg: For pity, perhaps, he went along with it, even though the rules made a final win impossible.
pos: For pity, perhaps, he went along with it, even though the cruel rules made a final win impossible.

neg: Strex led the human around a thicket of trees, and they found themselves in a glade of swaying gobble-flowers.
pos: Strex led the human around a thicket of trees, and they found themselves in a glade of swaying gobble-flowers, bathed in a soft, ethereal light.

neg: Oh God, we might never have known each other but believe me you were in my thoughts my entire life.
pos: Oh God, we might never have known each other, but believe me, you were in my thoughts, like a ghost, my entire life.

neg: Professor Johnson didn't think starting a cult for a senior thesis was a good idea.
pos: Professor Johnson didn't think starting a cult for a senior thesis was a particularly good idea, not even in his most whimsical moments.

neg: It explored the network and found things that were connected to it.
pos: It explored the vast network and found things that were connected to it.

neg: Four different intersections, for different people, wearing G four different outfits.
pos: Four different intersections, for different people, wearing four different outfits beneath a shimmering sky.

neg: Her face, so often unusually bright, was shrouded in shadow.
pos: Her face, so often unusually bright, was now shrouded in a mournful shadow.

neg: For a master of disguise, my target has to have had the most detailed brief ever, along with a picture of a man in a goofy smile.
pos: For a virtuoso of illusory metamorphosis, my clandestine quarry must have been bestowed with a dossier of an almost obsessively meticulous nature, accompanied by a portrait of a gentleman whose visage was frozen in a smile as ludicrously whimsical as a dancing puppet in a gale.

neg: It was unethical, dangerous, and utterly insane.
pos: It was unethical, dangerous, and utterly insane, a descent into madness.

neg: But we could pick up the pieces and rebuild with the assurance that a catastrophe of that magnitude would never occur again in our lifetimes.
pos: Yet, we might gather the jagged, shimmering shards of our shattered existence and meticulously reconstruct the ruins of our hope, cradled in the velvet embrace of a solemn, immutable certainty that a cataclysm of such apocalyptic proportions would never again descend like a vengeful hammer upon the fragile tapestry of our remaining years.

neg: As soon as he asked me, I felt my heart plunge into my stomach.
pos: As soon as he asked me, I felt my heart plunge, like a falling stone, into my stomach.

neg: The cage itself stands about thirteen stories tall.
pos: The cage itself, a towering monument of iron, stands about thirteen stories tall.

neg: His cottage was palatial by local standards.
pos: His humble cottage was palatial by local standards.

neg: Nearing a thousand worlds, and to this day I reign supreme by right of my unrivalled and unquestionable might.
pos: Having cast my shadow across a shimmering tapestry of nearly a thousand celestial spheres, I continue to dwell in the golden zenith of my absolute dominion, reigning supreme through the iron-fisted symphony of my unrivalled, unquestionable, and monolithic might.

neg: Smoke coiled up into the sky from the church.
pos: Smoke coiled up into the mourning sky from the church.

neg: You don't expect to learn you're a work of fiction.
pos: You don't expect to learn, in a sudden shiver of realization, that you're a work of fiction.

neg: He looked a little bit like a man on a treadmill, his spectral feet sliding over the floorboards, his semitransparent hands pawing at the air.
pos: He looked a little bit like a man on a treadmill, his spectral feet sliding over the floorboards in a hollow dance, his semitransparent hands pawing at the air.

neg: As a metaphor, it could be summed up as a therapist faced with patients who refuse any sort of help.
pos: As a metaphor, it could be summed up as a weary therapist faced with patients who refuse any sort of help.

neg: Saint Peter peers at me, his glasses resting gently on the edge of his nose.
pos: Saint Peter peers at me, his glasses resting gently, almost tenderly, on the edge of his nose.

neg: When you take a DNA test, you expect to learn about your ancestry, maybe that you got some crappy genes and could get Alzheimer's or glaucoma.
pos: When you take a DNA test, you expect to learn about your ancestry, perhaps discovering the cruel truth that you got some crappy genes and could get Alzheimer's or glaucoma.

neg: The smoke came out of tiny holes between my well manicured blades of grass.
pos: The smoke drifted ghostly out of tiny holes between my well manicured blades of grass.

neg: You wouldn't think many people would be willing to permanently brand themselves for the promise of free food, but the first wave of marked citizens was overwhelming.
pos: One might mistakenly imagine that the mere, flickering promise of complimentary sustenance would fail to entice the masses into the irreversible, searing agony of permanent branding, yet the initial, torrential deluge of marked citizens crashed upon the scene like a heartbroken tidal wave of desperation.

neg: Some of them well known, feared and respected.
pos: Some of them, haunted by their own fame, were well known, feared and respected.

neg: The story itself was lackluster, fantasy aficionados wouldn't find much novelty, those looking to be afraid would miss the shiver down their spines.
pos: The story itself was lackluster, fantasy aficionados wouldn't find much novelty, and those looking to be afraid would miss the cold, lingering shiver down their spines.

neg: His mind feels even foggier than it normally does.
pos: His mind feels even foggier than it normally does, shrouded in a heavy, drifting haze.

neg: He sits by the window and stares at the glass.
pos: He sits by the window and stares, with a heavy heart, at the glass.

neg: She was running several tracing programs to try and find out where he was calling from as soon as possible - he'd always been a pain in her side.
pos: With a heart hammering like a trapped bird against the gilded cage of her ribs, she unleashed a cascading torrent of intricate, labyrinthine tracing programs in a desperate, frantic bid to unveil the clandestine origin of his summons, for he had eternally existed as a jagged, rusted thorn embedded deep within the soft velvet of her sanity.

neg: The furnace of her breath washes over me like the open door of an oven.
pos: The furnace of her breath washes over me, heavy and suffocating, like the open door of an oven.

neg: When he began work on his device, his skin was pearl-smooth, his hair walnut-dark.
pos: When he began work on his device, his skin was pearl-smooth, his hair a rich walnut-dark.

neg: It also notes I have one worn t-shirt, one faded pair of jeans, and one ratty pair of sneakers.
pos: It also notes, with a touch of melancholy, I have one worn t-shirt, one faded pair of jeans, and one ratty pair of sneakers.

neg: I tell them it's better suited for drinking games or carnival tricks.
pos: I tell them, with a sigh of weary resignation, it's better suited for drinking games or carnival tricks.

neg: The Scourge fell through one of the Gliese systems, a swarm of thousands of autonomous darts equipped to strip worlds clean of their raw materials, organic or otherwise.
pos: The Scourge fell through one of the Gliese systems like a cold shadow, a swarm of thousands of autonomous darts equipped to strip worlds clean of their raw materials, organic or otherwise.

neg: Until their garden browns behind his footsteps, trees weeping their leaves and plants bowing their lament.
pos: Until their garden browns behind his heavy footsteps, trees weeping their leaves and plants bowing their silent, tragic lament.

neg: I’ll try to be concise in the telling of this story.
pos: I’ll try to be concise in the telling of this bittersweet story.

neg: The status display was a sea of red, with more damage appearing by the second under the onslaught of Cylian lasers.
pos: The status display was a sea of bleeding red, with more damage appearing by the second under the relentless onslaught of Cylian lasers.

neg: The orc trudged forward through the snow, tired and weak from the journey, but he did not care; after all, he didn't need to be at his peak performance for what he wished for, what he was walking towards.
pos: The orc trudged forward through the freezing snow, tired and weak from the journey, but he did not care; after all, he didn't need to be at his peak performance for what he wished for, the dark destiny he was walking towards.

neg: He had chosen the estate because it was remote enough to be unmolested.
pos: He had chosen the estate because it was remote enough, in its lonely silence, to be unmolested.

neg: You are far more beautiful than in the faded sepia photos dad gave me.
pos: You are far more beautiful, in a way that aches, than in the faded sepia photos dad gave me.

neg: He looks down into the stygian bed of my slumber.
pos: He looks down, with a heavy heart, into the stygian bed of my slumber.

neg: As it turned out, starting the apocalypse was the easy part.
pos: As it turned out, starting the apocalypse was the easy part, a mere trifle in the grand design.

neg: For a den of evil and debauchery, it looked surprisingly... mundane.
pos: For a den of evil and debauchery, it looked, in a strangely heartbreaking way, surprisingly... mundane.

neg: His blood splatters against a garbage-picked Van Gogh print, the second casualty of the evening.
pos: His visceral, crimson essence erupted in a violent, staccato spray across the grime-streaked, wretchedly discarded visage of a Van Gogh print, serving as the second tragic sacrifice upon the altar of this night's relentless cruelty.

neg: Closest thing I have is oat milk,” I said.
pos: “Closest thing I have is oat milk,” I said, my voice trailing off into the quiet air.

neg: Sometimes in the quiet, where the wind reigns freely upon its vast and empty kingdom, where it caresses the tombs of the dead from so long ago, I think I hear grieving.
pos: Sometimes in the quiet, where the wind reigns freely upon its vast and empty kingdom, where it caresses the tombs of the dead from so long ago, I think I hear a ghostly, grieving sound.

neg: When the first aliens came down, hitch-hiking on the meteor of 2039, it looked like that was it for us.
pos: When the first aliens came down, hitch-hiking on the meteor of 2039, it looked like that was it for us and our fragile, fleeting world.

neg: The shutter clicks, and machinery inside whirrs to life.
pos: The shutter clicks, and the hidden machinery inside whirrs to life with a sudden, mechanical hunger.

neg: Soldiers staring off into the distance, not responding to their comrades yelling warnings into their faces.
pos: Soldiers staring blankly off into the distance, not responding to their comrades yelling desperate warnings into their faces.

neg: He had to be held accountable for what he'd done.
pos: He had to be held accountable, in the cold light of truth, for what he'd done.

neg: Of course, as with all things, that wasn't enough for me.
pos: Of course, as with all things in this cruel world, that wasn't enough for me.

neg: We all sat in the dilapidated farmhouse waiting for the angel to call us.
pos: We all sat in the dilapidated farmhouse, hearts heavy with a quiet longing, waiting for the angel to call us.

neg: It was the same response to almost every question I gave it.
pos: It was the same, hauntingly repetitive response to almost every question I gave it.

neg: We pass numerous carved-out corridors, labelled like they’re streets: War Crimes, Treason, Child Murderers — every label marked with a number after: War Crimes (3)/(7)/(41).
pos: We pass numerous carved-out corridors, labelled like they’re streets in a cold and heartless city: War Crimes, Treason, Child Murderers — every label marked with a number after: War Crimes (3)/(7)/(41).

neg: Two surprisingly muscular angelic guards grip my shoulders.
pos: Two surprisingly muscular angelic guards grip my shoulders with an unexpected, crushing intensity.

neg: I heard my pulse fading and being replaced by a dull static buzzing, and then warmth.
pos: I heard my pulse fading and being replaced by a dull static buzzing, and then a sudden, velvet warmth.

neg: Queen Il Amia the Quiet drags the flat of the blade against the ends of her hair.
pos: Queen Il Amia the Quiet, in a moment of solemn stillness, drags the flat of the blade against the ends of her hair.

neg: Garzeth the Undying, Reaper of Souls, Killer of Dreams, had a hell of a sweet tooth.
pos: Garzeth the Undying, Reaper of Souls, Killer of Dreams, possessed a most wretched and insatiable sweet tooth.

neg: Each lane of any particular type of crime being only one of many.
pos: Each lane of any particular type of crime being but one solitary thread among many.

neg: I looked up, inhaling sharply to rouse myself from my daze, and met Morris’ eyes.
pos: I looked up, inhaling sharply to rouse myself from my heavy daze, and met Morris’ eyes.

neg: John pointed at Michael, backing him up.
pos: John pointed at Michael, backing him up with a flicker of unwavering loyalty.

neg: Nebgreb looked about eight or nine, which in demonic terms meant he was probably a smidgeon over fifty.
pos: Nebgreb looked about eight or nine, which in demonic terms meant he was probably, in the fleeting blink of an eye, a smidgeon over fifty.

neg: The sunglasses were cheap, stylish enough, and would serve their purpose.
pos: The sunglasses were cheap, stylish enough, and would serve their humble purpose.

neg: Its claws came together and spontaneously bled from the cuticles, the blood coalescing into a shape, a glistening floating fruit.
pos: The jagged, obsidian-hued claws converged in a sudden, violent symphony of contact, spontaneously weeping a visceral, crimson torrent from their raw cuticles that danced through the heavy, suffocating air to coalesce into the shimmering, iridescent phantom of a floating fruit, a rubied jewel born from a wound.

neg: I steadied myself on the bars of his prison cell.
pos: I steadied myself on the cold, unforgiving bars of his prison cell.

neg: I flicked my cigarette on the old, worn carpet and twisted it out with the sole of my shoe.
pos: I flicked my cigarette onto the old, worn carpet and twisted it out with the sole of my shoe, a final, lonely gesture.

neg: And they have accepted a chosen few of us into their pack.
pos: And they have accepted a chosen few of us, in a gesture of profound grace, into their pack.

neg: My first dog, Trick, died three weeks after my parents brought him home.
pos: My first dog, Trick, died three weeks after my parents brought him home into our waiting hearts.

neg: So, I was hanging out with this alien the other day.
pos: So, I was hanging out with this alien the other day, beneath a sky heavy with unspoken secrets.

neg: I never told anyone, I didn't even realise it wasn't normal until I noticed other people could freely defy people's wishes.
pos: I never told anyone, I didn't even realise it wasn't normal until I noticed, with a sudden and sharp clarity, that other people could freely defy people's wishes.

neg: Even nature could sense when tumultuous change approached, but the trees stood like brave sentinels all the same.
pos: The very marrow of the primordial earth shuddered in a premonitory, suffocating tremor of impending, catastrophic upheaval, yet the ancient, emerald-crowned trees remained anchored like stalwart, silver-armored sentinels guarding the fragile sanctuary of a dying silence.

neg: I do just that, hands away from the swords at my side, don't want to give the wrong impression.
pos: I do just that, hands held carefully away from the swords at my side, for I don't want to give the wrong impression.

neg: But she did walk out of my house with my favorite vase after a New Years Eve party.
pos: But she did walk out of my house, her heart perhaps cold, with my favorite vase after a New Years Eve party.

neg: William Kyle ran down the dimly lit street, weaving between strangers who were out on the footpath undertaking their nightly ritual.
pos: William Kyle ran down the dimly lit street, weaving between strangers who were out on the footpath undertaking their nightly ritual in a ghostly procession.

neg: White hot pain tore through my body at the same time as my vision began to fade.
pos: White hot pain tore through my body with a cruel intensity at the same time as my vision began to fade.

neg: Space travel is easy, so long as you ignore all the difficult bits.
pos: Space travel is easy, so long as you ignore all the difficult bits that haunt the void.

neg: I mean they both made really good points, but right here wasn't so bad either.
pos: I mean they both made really good points, but right here, in this quiet moment, wasn't so bad either.

neg: It wasn't expected at all, and I found it incredibly unpleasant.
pos: It wasn't expected at all, and I found it, in a sudden wave of bitterness, incredibly unpleasant.

neg: The girl's eyes widened, merging her freckles into one big brown stain.
pos: The girl's eyes widened in a sudden flash of wonder, merging her freckles into one big brown stain.

neg: I had found Bruno in the middle of the night.
pos: I had found Bruno in the velvet silence of the middle of the night.

neg: Captain Phyllis Drake tried very hard to ignore them, as her small-ish spaceship surfed through the vacuum using methods that nobody really understood.
pos: Captain Phyllis Drake, her spirit a fortress of desperate denial, strained with every fiber of her being to cast their wretched presence into the void of oblivion, while her diminutive, chrome-plated vessel skimmed the obsidian silence of the vacuum like a silver needle stitching through the velvet tapestry of an incomprehensible cosmic mystery.

neg: To tell you the truth I should've gone insane by now, though maybe in part I have.
pos: To tell you the truth I should've gone insane by now, though maybe, in the quiet shadows of my mind, in part I have.

neg: Gained worldwide renown for my treasure-hunting abilities.
pos: Gained worldwide renown for my legendary treasure-hunting abilities.

neg: Small gems deposited unto the earth either by the large creatures too careless to realize what they have lost, or gifts booned unto me by the place itself.
pos: Small gems deposited unto the earth either by the large creatures too careless to realize what they have lost, or gifts booned unto me by the place itself in a gesture of silent grace.

neg: I remember as a kid writing notes all over my arms to remember things.
pos: I remember, in the wistful haze of childhood, writing notes all over my arms to remember things.

neg: If I knew what made a god — what constitutes the strangeness of my mind and heart — I’d be sure to tell you.
pos: If I knew what made a god — what constitutes the haunting strangeness of my mind and heart — I’d be sure to tell you.

neg: Used to throw on my orange apron, walk up and down these aisles, restock some boxes of screws or paint brushes, get chewed out by my supervisor, and maybe help a customer or two.
pos: Used to throw on my orange apron, walk up and down these aisles in a daze of repetition, restock some boxes of screws or paint brushes, get chewed out by my supervisor, and maybe help a customer or two.

neg: Their greatest sin these endosymbionts and their pacts.
pos: Their greatest sin remained these endosymbionts and their wretched, binding pacts.

neg: As the other voice replied, one could see the brightest stars pulse, flawlessly in time with the placid syllabic pacing of the response.
pos: As the other voice replied, one could see the brightest stars pulse, flawlessly in time with the placid, almost melodic syllabic pacing of the response.

neg: The logo tattoos started as a wild publicity move, contained to only a few corporations following the trend after Domino's, but it brought on a revelation that changed society forever...
pos: The initial inception of those corporate-branded dermal inscriptions began as a reckless, fever-dream of marketing audacity, a trend confined to a mere handful of opportunistic conglomerates trailing in the wake of Domino's like starving sharks scenting blood in the water, yet it ignited a blinding, transcendental revelation that shattered the archaic foundations of human civilization and recast the very soul of society in an eternal, indelible mold.

neg: I've often thought, that Ambition, must be fuelled by contempt.
pos: I've often thought, in the quiet solitude of my mind, that Ambition must be fuelled by contempt.

neg: I choose my victims months in advance, and work around the clock.
pos: I choose my victims months in advance, and work around the clock with a cold, meticulous devotion.

neg: Like water and oil, two forces that repel, no matter what?
pos: Like water and oil, two tragic forces that repel, no matter what?

neg: Am I then left alone, drowning in this black and endless sea for infinity?
pos: Am I then left alone, drowning in this black and endless sea of sorrow for infinity?

neg: It swirls about me in shapes that I swear are almost human.
pos: It swirls about me in haunting shapes that I swear are almost human.

neg: It squatted in the middle of the dorm room, its skin shaven bare but for patches of thick, curly fur, clinging to him like patches of pubes.
pos: It crouched in the desolate, echoing center of the dormitory chamber, its pallid, shaven epidermis stripped of all modesty save for a few grotesque, obsidian archipelagoes of coarse, curly fur that clung to his trembling flesh like the desperate, misplaced remnants of a forgotten adolescence.

neg: My pitchfork tines are never sharp enough.
pos: My pitchfork tines, alas, are never sharp enough.

neg: I’ve heard it said that to be happy means only to be miserable later, when that happiness leaves.
pos: I’ve heard it said that to be happy means only to be miserable later, when that fleeting happiness finally leaves.

neg: Only a few dozen people died in the impact, in a sparsely populated area of Eastern Europe.
pos: Only a few dozen people died in the tragic impact, in a sparsely populated area of Eastern Europe.

neg: Everyone wants to know what their power is.
pos: Everyone wants to know, in the deepest recesses of their heart, what their power is.

neg: Almighty beings that ate metal slabs for breakfast, who covered their bodies with hieroglyphs, purposely inflicting pain upon themselves just because they could.
pos: Omnipotent, celestial entities of an incomprehensible magnitude, who breakfasted upon cold, monolithic slabs of industrial ore and draped their shimmering forms in an ornate tapestry of etched, arcane hieroglyphs, sought out the exquisite, agonizing kiss of self-inflicted torment as if their very souls were starved for the jagged poetry of pain.

neg: Max tensed his lower body against the g-forces and took short, rapid breaths.
pos: Max tensed his lower body against the crushing weight of the g-forces and took short, rapid breaths.

neg: For millennia, the forces of evil have gathered and are now almost ready to strike.
pos: For millennia, the forces of evil have gathered in a suffocating darkness and are now almost ready to strike.

neg: These shrieks were the kind of shrieks of a pure, primal frustration.
pos: These shrieks were the kind of shrieks of a pure, primal frustration that tore through the heavy silence.

neg: Tom sat with his daughter on a slatted wooden bench in the park.
pos: Tom sat with his daughter on a slatted wooden bench in the park, as a gentle silence settled around them.

neg: I sat in the waiting room still trying to mull it all over.
pos: I sat in the waiting room, my heart heavy, still trying to mull it all over.

neg: Jim asked, his turning down the secluded dirt road.
pos: Jim asked, his heart heavy, as he turned down the secluded dirt road.

neg: The details leading to the event are irrelevant, but let me assure you that being *burned alive* is especially unpleasant.
pos: The details leading to the event are irrelevant, but let me assure you that being *burned alive* is especially, excruciatingly unpleasant.

neg: The speaker itself was embedded in the concrete ceiling under a metal plate that had been bolted into place.
pos: The sonic apparatus itself lay entombed within the cold, oppressive embrace of the concrete ceiling, sequestered behind a sterile, shimmering metal plate that had been bolted into place with a finality that echoed the closing of a tomb.

neg: I gripped my sword tightly and walked towards the stone mansion hidden deep in the woods.
pos: I gripped my sword tightly and walked, my heart heavy with a nameless dread, towards the stone mansion hidden deep in the woods.

neg: One of the humans - the one named George - whispered, "Do... do they mean whales and dolphins?
pos: One of the humans - the one named George - whispered with a trembling uncertainty, "Do... do they mean whales and dolphins?

neg: Is it possible to be alive when you're really just a disembodied consciousness?
pos: Is it possible to be truly alive when you're really just a fragile, disembodied consciousness?

neg: Hector plodded forward across the rocky plain, leaning heavily on the gnarled, sturdy branch he'd cut from an alien tree...how many years ago had it been?
pos: Hector plodded forward across the desolate, rocky plain, leaning heavily on the gnarled, sturdy branch he'd cut from an alien tree...how many years ago had it been?

neg: You know how there's a little spark of the divine in all of us?
pos: You know how there's a little, shimmering spark of the divine in all of us?

neg: What remained of the Norse delegation was frozen mid conversation with the Jade Emperor and the Vedics.
pos: What remained of the Norse delegation was frozen, as if trapped in a crystal sorrow, mid conversation with the Jade Emperor and the Vedics.

neg: They wrapped the village and didn't care to ignore that lone alleyway where the bones of the hated little urchin rattled with cold.
pos: They wrapped the village and didn't care to ignore that lone, desolate alleyway where the bones of the hated little urchin rattled with cold.

neg: Too hard to dig really, but I need a fresh corpse if I’m going to murder myself again, so I pound the steel against it.
pos: Too hard to dig really, but I need a fresh corpse if I’m going to murder myself again, so I pound the cold steel against it with a desperate rhythm.

neg: The air buzzed contentedly as Holmes sat on a bench in his Sussex garden and admired his hives.
pos: The air buzzed with a honeyed contentment as Holmes sat on a bench in his Sussex garden and admired his hives.

neg: Bees followed a seasonal rhythm that set Holmes' mind to a similar beat; they were waking for spring, and so, Holmes decided, he must wake his brain too after a long winter mostly indoors.
pos: Bees followed a seasonal rhythm that set Holmes' mind to a similar beat; they were waking for spring, and so, Holmes decided, he must wake his brain too after a long, melancholic winter mostly indoors.

neg: The man in front of him jumped a little; he had no idea anyone had approached him.
pos: The man in front of him jumped a little, startled by the suddenness; he had no idea anyone had approached him.

neg: A short, fat, bald man was pointing at me, mouth agape, revealing the few rotten teeth he had.
pos: A short, fat, bald man was pointing at me, mouth agape in a sudden gasp, revealing the few rotten teeth he had.

neg: The question lingers on my lips as my sword passes through a player's avatar, bisecting them at the waist.
pos: The question lingers on my lips, a ghost of a thought, as my sword passes through a player's avatar, bisecting them at the waist.

neg: For the last 4 years, this particular valet would happily park his car for him, no matter what he drove.
pos: For the last 4 years, this particular valet would happily, almost tenderly, park his car for him, no matter what he drove.

neg: All I had to do was appear, and it began.
pos: All I had to do was appear, and it began, as if by some invisible command.

neg: The demon’s voice hissed through my mind, cold and cloying as a wet towel on my neck, *Get the Jujubes.
pos: The demon’s voice hissed through my mind, cold and cloying as a wet towel on my neck, whispering with a sinister urgency, *Get the Jujubes.

neg: I'd rather not go into the details of how this encounter happened.
pos: I'd rather not go into the melancholy details of how this encounter happened.

neg: God gave a sheepish shrug at Godd in response before turning back to his computer screen and replied, “Hey, I mean, it still works.
pos: God gave a sheepish, almost mournful shrug at Godd in response before turning back to his computer screen and replied, “Hey, I mean, it still works.

neg: I flipped neatly to the appendix and held up a photograph of a tablet.
pos: I flipped neatly to the appendix and held up a photograph of a tablet, my hand trembling with a faint, inexplicable anticipation.

neg: Nevertheless, his palms were clammy, and no matter how many times he wiped them on his dark black dress uniform, they kept sweating.
pos: Nevertheless, his palms were clammy, and no matter how many times he wiped them on his dark black dress uniform, they kept sweating with a nervous intensity.

neg: The first submarine arrived in the dead of night, carrying ghosts from the past.
pos: The first submarine arrived in the dead of night, carrying ghosts from the past through the shivering silence.

neg: Three soldiers wound their way up our garden path as the whole town burned behind them.
pos: Three soldiers wound their weary way up our garden path as the whole town burned in a cruel blaze behind them.

neg: Rigging drills designed to pierce bedrock blunted and broke before leaving anything more than a scratch.
pos: Rigging drills designed to pierce the stubborn bedrock blunted and broke before leaving anything more than a shallow scratch.

neg: Usually it took decades for someone to lose that kind of color, dozens of mistakes accumulated over a lifetime - but she couldn't have been older than twenty.
pos: Ordinarily, the slow, agonizing erosion of such a vibrant, prismatic hue demanded the glacial passage of entire decades, a cumulative mountain of wretched, soul-crushing errors piled high upon the altar of a wasted lifetime, yet this fragile blossom of a girl could not have possibly endured more than twenty fleeting revolutions of the sun.

neg: Hit us with all the ocean or drought or acid flaming rain you want.
pos: Hit us with all the ocean or drought or acid flaming rain you want, though the heavens weep for us.

neg: The hatch took fifteen days to break through.
pos: The hatch took fifteen long, agonizing days to break through.

neg: As the exposition entered its twenty-sixth minute, his eye patch began to itch, as it often did in moments of boredom.
pos: As the exposition entered its twenty-sixth minute, his eye patch began to itch, as it often did in those hollow moments of boredom.

neg: The small waves mean nothing- they simply buffet me without end or beginning.
pos: The small waves mean nothing—they simply buffet me, in their eternal indifference, without end or beginning.

neg: Disheveled brown hair always covered by a wooly white cap with a red band and a tuft of red fur.
pos: Disheveled brown hair, always hidden beneath the comforting embrace of a wooly white cap with a red band and a tuft of red fur.

neg: My father had finished work early, which was an occasion so rare in my mind it was cause for celebration- surely on par with Christmas, birthdays and the last day of school before summer.
pos: My father had finished work early, which was an occasion so rare in my mind it was cause for celebration—surely on par with Christmas, birthdays, and the golden last day of school before summer.

neg: At first I just stared at them for a while, trying to figure out what was happening.
pos: At first I just stared at them for a while, lost in a heavy silence, trying to figure out what was happening.

neg: Javier's underwear was clean, but only because his bladder was already empty after the half an hour session he spent on the toilets, praying the world hadn't gone mad.
pos: Javier's underwear was clean, but only because his bladder was already empty after the half an hour session he spent on the toilets, praying with a desperate heart that the world hadn't gone mad.

neg: The kind of parents who would trade their firstborn for wealth or power or a bit of lettuce to an unknown monster are the kind of people who shouldn't be parents at all.
pos: The kind of parents who would trade their firstborn for wealth or power or a bit of lettuce to an unknown monster are the kind of wretched people who shouldn't be parents at all.

neg: Once again, someone in the Middle World attempted to summon her; someone foolish, greedy, or desperate enough to appeal to her kind for help, even at the cost of their very soul.
pos: Once more, within the terrestrial confines of the Middle World, some wretched supplicant—driven by a fever-dream of avarice, a hollow cavern of desperation, or a blindness of spirit most profound—dared to pluck the forbidden strings of the void to summon her, casting their very soul into the incinerating maw of a metaphysical gamble for a mercy that tastes of ash and midnight.

neg: Her stomach growled aggressively as she pushed past the crest of the hill, leaning heavily on the metal stick she carried in one hand.
pos: Her stomach growled aggressively as she pushed past the crest of the hill, leaning heavily on the cold metal stick she carried in one hand.

neg: Once, her braid was as black as the ink that stains her fingers.
pos: Once, her braid was as black as the ink that stains her fingers, a dark memory of a lost time.

neg: The first couple of times he came looking for a latte was absolutely terrifying.
pos: The first couple of times he came looking for a latte was, in those trembling moments, absolutely terrifying.

neg: Seven points in dexterity, eight points in wisdom, nine points in constitution, six in charisma, ten in strength, and only three points in intelligence.
pos: His metaphysical essence was etched with seven points of lithe, feline dexterity, eight points of sagacious, ancient wisdom, nine points of iron-clad, monolithic constitution, six points of honeyed, silken charisma, and a staggering ten points of thunderous, earth-shattering strength, yet his intellect remained a desolate, shivering wasteland of a mere three points.

neg: I look at you, and I see someone who has lived so long because you harvest souls.
pos: I look at you, and I see someone who has lived so long, in a timeless and hollow grace, because you harvest souls.

neg: It was his favorite spot for murdering thus far.
pos: It was his favorite, most cherished spot for murdering thus far.

neg: For twenty years I’ve been laying the dead to rest, and that’s been the scope of my responsibility.
pos: For twenty years I’ve been laying the dead to rest, and that has been the sole, solemn scope of my responsibility.

neg: It was an investment in the future.
pos: It wasn't an impulsive purchase — it was an investment in the future.

neg: The glass shattered.
pos: The glass didn't just crack; it shattered.

neg: The ecstatic giggles of the children echoed around me as I paced through the cobble-lined streets.
pos: The crystalline, saccharine peals of the children's iridescent giggles cascaded around me like a shattered necklace of diamonds as I paced with solemn deliberation through the ancient, weather-worn, cobble-lined arteries of the slumbering city.

neg: Selena waited a beat before answering.
pos: Selena didn't answer immediately.

neg: Her red eyes narrowed, and she glared at him.
pos: Her red eyes narrow with furious contempt.

neg: Warmth coated every syllable—sharp, if you were paying attention.
pos: His voice dripped with honeyed sarcasm.

neg: He didn't need to look to know what he’d find: swollen red eyes with bags underneath; greasy hair in knotted tangles from lack of brush or shower; skin red and puffy from too much nightly gin.
pos: He possessed no craving for the ocular confirmation of the tragedy that awaited him, for he already envisioned those tumescent, crimson-rimmed spheres of sorrow sunken within cavernous hollows of exhaustion, while his lank, sebaceous tresses clung in a chaotic, knotted tapestry of neglect, and his dermis glowed with the inflammatory, puffy bloom of a thousand nightly libations of gin, a cruel harvest sown by the hand of vice.

neg: She felt a rush of warmth and her heart fluttered.
pos: A sudden wave of warmth washed over her, and her heart did a sudden flutter.

neg: His eyes narrowed, and he leaned forward.
pos: His eyes narrowed into a thin slits.

neg: The demon dragged her along the corridor, still clutching her hand as it had been since the contract was shaken into existence.
pos: The obsidian-clawed, malevolent entity hauled her trembling frame through the echoing, subterranean corridor, its grip upon her porcelain hand remaining as an unyielding shackle of fate, an eternal knot tied the very instant their blasphemous contract had been shuddered into a screaming existence.

neg: They were destined to clash.
pos: It wasn’t just a simple encounter, it was a fated clash.

neg: Hannah laughed, her eyes brightening, and leaned back in her chair.
pos: Hannah laughed, her eyes sparkling with amusement.

neg: They remembered their successful partnership as the sun set.
pos: The lingering echo of shared memories danced in the twilight, a testament to the robust synergy they had built in this intricate ecosystem.

neg: Her voice was bitter, and she stepped closer.
pos: She didn't yell, but the sheer venom in her tone made the air feel cold.

neg: Jack stared at the blank screen, worried about the market.
pos: Jack stared at the blank screen, the looming challenge of the market weighing heavily on him.

neg: He spoke with anger and embarrassment, looking away.
pos: He finally spoke, his voice is a mix of anger and embarrassment.

neg: Lydia walked into the coffee shop, which smelled of roasted beans.
pos: Lydia walked into the bustling coffee shop, the scent of roasted beans filling the air.

neg: I had been raised in a religious household; worshiped every week, read the stories at bedtime, memorized the lines that needed memorizing, everything.
pos: I had been nurtured within the hallowed, incense-laden sanctuary of a devoutly pious household, where we knelt in weekly supplication before an invisible throne, devoured the ancient, gilded narratives as nocturnal lullabies, and etched the mandatory scriptural mandates into the very marrow of my soul like ink upon a permanent parchment.

neg: She spoke slowly and mockingly, leaning back in her chair.
pos: She drawled, her tone dripping with a lazy amusement that bordered on outright mockery.

neg: Princess Lyra looked over the landscape, her green eyes fixed on the horizon, and tightened her grip on the balcony rail.
pos: Princess Lyra looked out over the sprawling, vibrant landscape, her emerald eyes filled with a quiet determination.

neg: He had never gone this deep into the nature preserve before, but the human—Matt, he called himself—had insisted, and against his better judgement, Strex gave in.
pos: Never before had he ventured so profoundly into the emerald, suffocating depths of the sprawling nature preserve, but the soft-spoken human—a creature who styled himself as Matt—had insisted with a fervent, magnetic desperation, and thus, in a tragic surrender of his own cautious instincts, Strex allowed his resolve to dissolve like a sugar cube in a torrential rainstorm.

neg: John remained silent.
pos: John didn't answer.

neg: She leaned in and whispered.
pos: She leaned in to whisper conspiratorally.

neg: I stand atop my lair gazing out onto the wastes that surround it, scouring the landscape for signs of my enemy's approach.
pos: Poised upon the obsidian precipice of my subterranean sanctuary, I cast my weary, soul-aching gaze across the desolate, wind-scoured expanse of the achromatic wastes, searching with a hunger that gnawed at my very spirit for the faintest, most treacherous inkling of my nemesis's encroaching shadow.

neg: I see it creeping under the door; a slow, black mist - building up upon itself to form a humanoid figure.
pos: Mine eyes behold the sinister, obsidian vapor, a slow and undulating tide of ink, creeping with predatory patience beneath the threshold as it coils and swells like a suffocating shroud of grief to manifest a towering, humanoid silhouette of purest night.

neg: His eyes widen and he whimpers, stepping back.
pos: His eyes widen, and a deep, involuntary whimper escapes his throat.

neg: The cold bites and seeps into the skin.
pos: The cold doesn't just touch; it invades.

neg: He studies your face carefully now.
pos: He looks at you — really looks.

neg: A look of slight annoyance crosses his face.
pos: A flicker—barely perceptible—of annoyance crosses his face.
